hypothesis: If a Transformer-based encoder-decoder language model is augmented with a spectral alignment mechanism that reconstructs pure component spectra from mixture IR spectral representations during training, then it will more accurately predict the molecular structures of individual components present in IR spectra of mixtures compared to models without this alignment constraint, because enforcing reconstruction of pure component spectra forces the encoder to disentangle overlapping spectral contributions and learn chemically meaningful latent representations of individual compounds.
